commonProblems.h2: Mitsubishi Endeavor Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Mitsubishi Endeavor's sole 6G75 3.8L V6 petrol engine is generally robust but faces age-related failures at high mileage. Timing belt neglect and oil starvation are the dominant causes of catastrophic failure. Every rebuilt 6G75 engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded tensioners and seals designed to address original failure points - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty.

problems.h4: Mitsubishi Endeavor 6G75 Timing Belt Failure - Why Interference Engines Fail Catastrophically
problems.affectedModels: Mitsubishi Endeavor LS, XLS, SE, Limited (2004-2011, all 6G75-SOHC & 6G75-24V)
problems.typicalFailureMileage: 60,000-100,000 miles (if belt not replaced per schedule)
problems.rootCause: The 6G75 is an interference engine; a snapped timing belt causes pistons to strike open valves, bending both and destroying the cylinder head.

repairOptions.tier: Preventative Belt Replacement - Full kit with tensioner & water pump
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace timing belt, tensioner, idlers, water pump, seals
repairOptions.longevity: Prevents catastrophic failure; recommended every 60k miles

repairOptions.tier: Cylinder Head Repair - Valve replacement & machining
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove head, replace bent valves, machine surface, reassemble
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if block/pistons undamaged; labour-intensive

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt 6G75 Engine - Supply & fit
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove failed engine, install rebuilt long block with upgraded components, test & commission
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for catastrophic damage; includes 12-month warranty

problems.recommendation: If your Endeavor has over 60k miles and no timing belt service record, budget for preventative replacement now. If the belt has snapped, a rebuilt long block is almost always more cost-effective than head repair on a 15+ year old import.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt 6G75 engine prices for your Mitsubishi Endeavor

problems.h4: Mitsubishi Endeavor 6G75 Rod Knock - Bottom End Failure from Oil Starvation
problems.affectedModels: Mitsubishi Endeavor LS, XLS, SE, Limited (2004-2011, all 6G75 variants)
problems.rootCause: Low oil pressure or sludge buildup starves crankshaft bearings of lubrication, causing bearing wear, knock, and eventual rod failure.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Oil System Flush - Sludge removal & fresh oil
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Chemical flush, oil/filter change, pressure test
repairOptions.longevity: Only if knock is very early stage; not a repair for audible knock

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Short Block Rebuild - Bearings & rings
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove engine, replace crank bearings, piston rings, oil pump
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if heads are sound; requires specialist machining

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt 6G75 Long Block - Supply & fit
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Install rebuilt long block with upgraded bearings, seals, gaskets
repairOptions.longevity: Most reliable solution for audible knock; includes warranty

problems.recommendation: Once rod knock is audible, bearing damage is advanced. For a 2004-2011 Endeavor valued at £2,000-£4,000 UK, a rebuilt long block with warranty offers better long-term value than a partial rebuild.
problems.cta: Get quotes for 6G75 bottom end replacement Mitsubishi Endeavor

problems.h4: Mitsubishi Endeavor 6G75 Oil Consumption - Valve Seal & PCV Failure
problems.affectedModels: Mitsubishi Endeavor XLS, Limited, AWD variants (2007-2011, high-mileage 6G75-24V)
problems.rootCause: Hardened valve stem seals and clogged PCV system allow oil to enter combustion chambers, causing blue smoke and increased consumption.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - PCV & Seal Service - Replace PCV valve, top seals
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace PCV valve, valve cover gasket, external seals
repairOptions.longevity: May reduce consumption if caught early; non-invasive

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Valve Seal Replacement - Heads on-car
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove valve covers, replace valve stem seals with heads installed
repairOptions.longevity: Effective for moderate consumption; avoids full head removal

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt 6G75 Engine - Supply & fit
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Install rebuilt engine with upgraded seals, rings, PCV system
repairOptions.longevity: Best for severe consumption (>1L/1k miles) or combined failures

problems.recommendation: Start with PCV and external seal service. If consumption exceeds 1 litre per 1,000 miles after this, valve seal replacement or full rebuild becomes the sensible path.
problems.cta: Compare Mitsubishi Endeavor 6G75 oil consumption repair costs

problems.h4: Mitsubishi Endeavor 6G75 Lash Adjuster Ticking - Hydraulic Lifter Noise
problems.rootCause: Hydraulic lash adjusters lose pressure due to wear, sludge, or low oil pressure, causing persistent ticking from the valve train.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Oil Flush & Additive - Clean lifters in-situ
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Engine flush, high-detergent oil, lifter additive
repairOptions.longevity: May quieten noise if sludge-related; temporary fix

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Lash Adjuster Replacement - Valve cover off
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove valve cover, replace faulty hydraulic adjusters
repairOptions.longevity: Effective for isolated ticking; requires correct torque procedure

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt 6G75 Cylinder Head - Supply only
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Rebuilt head with new adjusters, valves, seals; swap with existing
repairOptions.longevity: Best for widespread adjuster failure or combined head issues

problems.recommendation: Ticking alone rarely warrants engine replacement. Try an oil flush and adjuster replacement first. Only consider a rebuilt head if ticking is accompanied by misfires or compression loss.
problems.cta: Find Mitsubishi Endeavor 6G75 lash adjuster repair specialists

problems.h4: Mitsubishi Endeavor Transmission Shift Issues - Often Mistaken for Engine Failure
problems.affectedModels: Mitsubishi Endeavor all trims (2004-2011, 4-speed Sportronic automatic)
problems.rootCause: Shift interlock solenoid failure (recall 08V139000) or worn transmission bands cause harsh shifts, stuck gears, or no-start conditions that mimic engine failure.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Shift Solenoid Replacement - Recall repair
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace shift interlock solenoid, update TCM software
repairOptions.longevity: Fixes stuck-in-park/no-start; recall may still apply

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Transmission Service - Fluid, filter, bands
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Drain/refill ATF, replace filter, adjust bands, road test
repairOptions.longevity: Restores shift quality if no internal damage present

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Transmission - Supply & fit
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove gearbox, install rebuilt unit with upgraded clutch packs
repairOptions.longevity: Required for slipped bands or burnt clutches; pairs with engine work

problems.recommendation: Always diagnose transmission before condemning the engine. A no-start or harsh shift is often solenoid or fluid-related. If transmission repair exceeds £1,500, consider combined engine/transmission rebuild for long-term reliability.
problems.cta: Compare Mitsubishi Endeavor transmission and engine repair quotes

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Mitsubishi Endeavor
finalCta.paragraph: The Mitsubishi Endeavor's 6G75 V6 is a capable engine when maintained, but age and mileage bring predictable failures. EngineMarket connects you with UK specialists who understand US-import Endeavors, offering rebuilt engines with upgraded components that address original weak points. With nationwide supply & fit and a 12-month unlimited mileage warranty, you can restore your Endeavor without main-dealer pricing. Compare quotes today and keep your import on the road.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Mitsubishi Endeavor Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Mitsubishi Endeavor and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineYears.h2: Mitsubishi Endeavor Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the Mitsubishi Endeavor's 2004-2011 production timeline, organised so UK import owners can quickly identify their 6G75 engine and compare rebuilt prices from specialists.

years.description: The Endeavor launched with the 6G75 3.8L V6 as its sole powertrain, establishing the platform for all subsequent model years.
keyChanges: Introduction of Mitsubishi's first dedicated mid-size crossover SUV for North America
Standard 6G75-SOHC 3.8L V6 paired with 4-speed Sportronic automatic
Available in LS and XLS trims, FWD or full-time AWD
Base MSRP positioned below competitors like Ford Explorer and Jeep Grand Cherokee
knownFor: Smooth, refined V6 power delivery with adequate torque for towing
Early examples show timing belt tensioner wear by 60k miles if not serviced
AWD system robust but adds weight impacting fuel economy
US-spec emissions hardware requires adaptation for UK compliance
years.cta: Get quotes for 2004-2006 Mitsubishi Endeavor replacement engines
years.ticker: Endeavor 6G75 timing belt replacement • Endeavor LS engine knock • XLS AWD oil consumption

years.description: The 2007 refresh brought updated styling, interior tweaks and minor engine calibration improvements for the 6G75 V6.
keyChanges: Revised front fascia, headlights and grille for modernised appearance
Interior updates including new seat fabrics and enhanced audio options
SE trim introduced as new mid-level option between XLS and Limited
Engine calibration tweaks for slightly improved emissions and throttle response
knownFor: Facelift models command slightly higher UK import values due to updated styling
Hydraulic lash adjuster ticking becomes more prevalent on 80k+ mile examples
Exhaust manifold cracks reported on high-mileage AWD models due to heat cycling
Shift interlock recall (08V139000) affects many 2007-2008 automatic transmissions
years.ticker: Endeavor SE engine misfire • Limited AWD overheating • 6G75 exhaust manifold crack

years.description: The Endeavor's final model years saw steady production with no major mechanical changes, making parts interchangeability excellent across the range.
keyChanges: No significant mechanical revisions; focus on trim and option updates
Limited edition packages offered in final year with unique badging and wheels
Production ended in 2011 as Mitsubishi shifted focus to Outlander and other global models
UK import availability increased as US owners upgraded to newer SUVs
knownFor: Excellent parts commonality across all 2004-2011 model years simplifies replacement
High-mileage examples (120k+ miles) show increased oil consumption and valve train noise
Final-year models often have more complete service records from original US owners
Strong enthusiast community supports DIY rebuilds with online technical resources
years.cta: Enquire about Mitsubishi Endeavor 2009-2011 engine availability
years.ticker: 2010 Endeavor engine no start • Limited valve cover leak • 6G75 compression test specs

faq.h2: Mitsubishi Endeavor Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Mitsubishi Endeavor engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: A rebuilt Mitsubishi Endeavor 6G75 engine typically costs £2,600-£3,200 supply-only in the UK, with supply & fit adding £800-£1,400 depending on garage location and drivetrain.
keyPoints: Reconditioned 6G75 long blocks start around £2,800 from UK specialists
Used donor engines range £1,800-£2,400 but carry higher risk
Supply & fit labour varies by garage expertise with US-spec imports
Always confirm warranty terms and core return policy before ordering
items.cta: Compare rebuilt 6G75 engine prices for your Mitsubishi Endeavor

items.question: Is the Mitsubishi Endeavor 6G75 engine reliable?
items.answer: The 6G75 V6 is fundamentally robust but requires strict timing belt maintenance; neglect leads to catastrophic interference engine damage at 60k-100k mile intervals.
keyPoints: SOHC 24-valve design is simple and durable when serviced correctly
Timing belt must be replaced every 60k miles with tensioner and water pump
High-mileage examples commonly develop oil consumption via valve seals
Lash adjuster ticking is frequent but rarely indicates imminent failure
items.cta: Find Mitsubishi Endeavor 6G75 reliability specialists UK

items.question: What causes Mitsubishi Endeavor engines to fail?
items.answer: Timing belt snapping, oil starvation leading to rod knock, and valve seal wear causing excessive oil consumption are the three most common Mitsubishi Endeavor 6G75 failure modes.
keyPoints: Snapped timing belt bends valves and pistons (interference design)
Infrequent oil changes cause bearing wear and audible bottom-end knock
PCV system clogging accelerates valve seal hardening and blue smoke
Exhaust manifold cracks on AWD models from repeated heat cycling
items.cta: Get quotes for Mitsubishi Endeavor 6G75 failure repair

items.question: Will any V6 engine fit my Mitsubishi Endeavor?
items.answer: Only the factory-fitted 6G75 3.8L V6 is a direct replacement for the Mitsubishi Endeavor; swapping other Mitsubishi V6 engines requires extensive modification and is not recommended.
keyPoints: 6G75 block, mounts, wiring and ECU are unique to the Endeavor platform
6G74 3.5L V6 lacks power and requires custom ECU tuning to function
Aftermarket crate engines exist but cost significantly more than rebuilt 6G75 units
Always verify engine code matches your VIN before ordering a replacement
items.cta: Compare Mitsubishi Endeavor 6G75 compatible engine options

items.question: How long does a Mitsubishi Endeavor 6G75 engine last?
items.answer: With proper maintenance, Mitsubishi Endeavor 6G75 engines regularly reach 150,000-200,000 miles; timing belt adherence is the single biggest factor in achieving high mileage.
keyPoints: Regular oil changes with correct viscosity prevent bearing wear
Timing belt replacement at 60k miles prevents catastrophic valve damage
Replacing valve seals around 100k miles reduces oil consumption
Many US examples exceed 200k miles with documented service history
items.cta: Enquire about high-mileage Mitsubishi Endeavor engine rebuilds

items.question: What is the Mitsubishi Endeavor timing belt replacement interval?
items.answer: Mitsubishi specifies timing belt replacement every 60,000 miles or 5 years for the Endeavor's 6G75 engine, whichever comes first, due to its interference design.
keyPoints: Always replace tensioner, idlers and water pump during belt service
Use OEM-spec belts; aftermarket variants have higher failure rates on 6G75
Failure to replace on schedule risks bent valves costing £2,000+ in repairs
Keep receipts as proof of service for warranty and resale purposes
items.cta: Find Mitsubishi Endeavor timing belt service specialists UK

items.question: Can I rebuild my Mitsubishi Endeavor 6G75 engine myself?
items.answer: Yes, the 6G75 is a straightforward SOHC V6 suitable for experienced DIY rebuilders, but requires precision machining, specialised tools and access to technical manuals.
keyPoints: Factory service manual and torque specs are essential for correct assembly
Cylinder block honing and crankshaft grinding require machine shop access
Timing belt installation demands precise alignment to avoid valve damage
Budget £800-£1,500 for quality rebuild kits beyond basic gasket sets
items.cta: Compare Mitsubishi Endeavor 6G75 rebuild kit suppliers UK

items.question: Where can I buy a Mitsubishi Endeavor engine in the UK?
items.answer: UK owners source Mitsubishi Endeavor 6G75 engines through specialist import breakers, online engine marketplaces, and US exporters who ship to UK ports with compliance documentation.
keyPoints: Verify supplier experience with US-spec emissions and ECU hardware
Request compression test results and service history for used engines
Confirm warranty covers UK road use and includes return shipping if faulty
Factor in import duties and VAT when ordering direct from US suppliers
items.cta: Get quotes from UK Mitsubishi Endeavor engine suppliers

items.question: Does the Mitsubishi Endeavor engine warranty cover UK road use?
items.answer: Most UK specialists offer 12-month unlimited mileage warranties on rebuilt Mitsubishi Endeavor 6G75 engines that explicitly cover UK road use and import compliance.
keyPoints: Confirm warranty terms cover both parts and labour for installation faults
Keep all service records to maintain warranty validity post-installation
Some suppliers require professional fitment for warranty activation
Warranty typically excludes damage from incorrect installation or misuse
items.cta: Compare Mitsubishi Endeavor engine warranty terms UK

items.question: Should I repair or replace my Mitsubishi Endeavor engine?
items.answer: For Mitsubishi Endeavor owners facing major 6G75 failures, a rebuilt engine with warranty often offers better long-term value than partial repairs on a 15+ year old import.
keyPoints: Catastrophic failures (belt snap, rod knock) almost always warrant full replacement
Minor issues (ticking, mild oil consumption) may be cost-effectively repaired
Consider the vehicle's UK market value (£2,000-£4,000) when budgeting repairs
Rebuilt engines with upgraded components reduce repeat failure risk
items.cta: Get tailored Mitsubishi Endeavor repair vs replace advice UK
